title: Cephaelis tenuiflora
taxonomy: Cephaelis tenuiflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephaelis, which consists of tropical plants native to Central and South America. The plant is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Cephaelis tenuiflora is a relatively rare species within its genus, and it is often found in shaded, humid environments. The plant's classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its morphology, anatomy, and genetic features.
uses: Cephaelis tenuiflora, also known as the tree of life, is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in South America and other parts of the world. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, which have been found to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. benefits: Cephaelis tenuiflora, also known as the yellow hawkweed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. parts: Cephaelis tenuiflora is also known as the Wild Coffee plant, which is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal parts include its roots and leaves. intro: Cephaelis tenuiflora, also known as Ipecacuanha or Quebra-Pedra, is a perennial plant native to South America, particularly in Brazil and Peru, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, the root of Cephaelis tenuiflora has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its emetic properties, which help to induce vomiting and relieve congestion. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids such as cephaeline and emetine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cephaelis tenuiflora is commonly prepared as a tincture or dried root, and is often used in traditional medicine, as well as in some pharmaceutical formulations.
